# Update & Advisory Agents

## Goals
- Users always know when there's a new Neotoma release relevant to them.
- Users hear about security advisories with severity-appropriate urgency.
- Critical security patches can apply themselves within minutes under tight predicates when user opts in.
- Every update decision is recorded as a Neotoma observation. Replayable forensics.
- Local-first: no central update server, no telemetry, no push delivery.

## Architecture

### Three sub-functions
**Daemon 1: release-watcher** — deterministic, no LLM. Polls npm and GitHub Releases. Records new releases as observations. Runs every 4 hours.

**Daemon 2: advisory-watcher** — deterministic, no LLM. Polls GitHub Security Advisories. Runs hourly when current; every 15 min when one minor behind; every 5 min when unfixed advisory affects installed version.

**Analyzer: release-analyzer** — LLM-driven, invoked when daemons record new findings.
- `release-analyzer-reader`: reads release notes / advisory text. Emits structured JSON.
- `release-analyzer-writer`: receives JSON, validates, stores as observation. Never sees raw notes text.

**update-applier** — deterministic code path, no LLM. Takes validated structured directive and executes. No LLM ever runs npm install.

### Entity types
- `release`: version, published_at, release_notes_url, npm_url, commit_sha, signed_by
- `advisory`: ghsa_id, severity, affected_versions, fixed_in, summary, published_at
- `release_analysis`: from_version, to_version, categories[], severity, migration_steps[], peer_compat_impact
- `update_decision`: release_id or advisory_id, decision (deferred/applied/skipped/auto_applied), decided_by, decided_at, outcome

### Source feeds
- npm registry for version-only checks
- GitHub Releases API for release notes (fetched only when new version detected)
- GitHub Security Advisories API for advisories

### Signature verification
- release-watcher verifies commit SHA in each release is signed by allowlisted GPG key before treating release as legitimate
- Allowlist bundled with Neotoma at install time
- Invalid signature: recorded as observation with signature_status:invalid, never proposed for application

## Autonomy ladder
- **Tier 0 — Silent observation** (always on): polling, recording, analyzing, running doctor checks. No notifications, no actions.
- **Tier 1 — Ambient notification** (default on): new minor/patch available → log entry in neotoma updates pending; after 30 days unacknowledged → single OS notification.
- **Tier 2 — Active notification** (default on): breaking changes, low/medium advisory, peer compat warning → OS notification with one-command upgrade.
- **Tier 3 — Urgent notification** (default on, cannot silence without explicit opt-out): high/critical advisory → persistent OS notification re-fires hourly + CLI banner on every neotoma invocation + security.unaddressed_advisory substrate flag.
- **Tier 4 — Auto-apply** (default OFF, explicit opt-in): fires only when ALL of: advisory severity critical + patch-level bump only + advisory signed by release key + doctor returns no risks + no schema migrations + peer compat preserved for all peers + NEOTOMA_AUTO_APPLY_CRITICAL_SECURITY_PATCHES=1 AND neotoma updates auto-apply enable + no auto-apply in last 24h.

## Phases
1. Release watcher and observation recording — deterministic daemon, new entity types seeded, signature verification, neotoma releases list CLI
2. Release analyzer and pending-updates surface — LLM reader/writer, release_analysis observations, Tier 1 notifications, neotoma updates apply/skip
3. Advisory watcher and urgent tier — advisory-watcher daemon, Tier 2/3 notifications, neotoma advisories list/show CLI
4. Auto-applier (Tier 4) — deterministic update-applier, pre-flight predicates, post-apply verification, auto-revert path, sandbox dogfood
5. Cross-instance fleet awareness — peer version observation via get_peer_status, per-instance advisory attribution, neotoma updates fleet CLI

## Open questions
- Where does the release signing key allowlist live (bundled vs hosted)?
- Auto-revert from botched update when one-way migration already ran — auto-applier refuses any release with migration markers
- Does Tier 3 urgent notification respect macOS Do Not Disturb? (time-sensitive interruption level for Tier 3)
- Pre-release / canary versions (out of scope for v1)
- Telemetry temptation — resist; local-first thesis is non-negotiable

## Risks
- Auto-apply blast radius — mitigated by predicate stack, rate limit, opt-in default off, auto-revert path
- Notification fatigue — mitigated by neotoma updates skip recording explicit per-version deferral
- Tampered release notes feeding analyzer false signals — reader/writer split confines impact to wrong analysis, not wrong code
- User disabling everything and running outdated install — harness [VERSION & SECURITY] rule surfaces version-age reminders in normal interactions
- Release-watcher polling load — negligible; npm endpoint cached aggressively, GitHub unauthenticated limit 60/hour
- Schema migrations from unexpected releases — analyzer flags migration burden in Tier 2; auto-apply predicate excludes any release with migrations

## Notes on composition
This is the third agent runtime targeting the same supervisor. After Phase 2, extract docs/subsystems/agent_runtime.md covering shared patterns (daemon supervision, reader/writer split, run-as-observation, autonomy budgets, OS notification tiers). Third runtime is the right moment; second was too early, fourth would be too late.
